channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Mangwe chiefs urge returnees from SA to rebuild their lives in Zimbabwe @thezimbabwean
item.description: Traditional leaders in Mangwe District, Matabeleland South, have urged Zimbabweans returning from South Africa not to lose hope but to embrace their new reality and rebuild their lives at home by learning from communities that have managed to survive despite the country’s economi #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwean

item.title: Drug and cybercrime surge fuels court backlog as JSC steps up recruitment @zimsituation
item.description: 25 new magistrates sworn-in Source: Drug and cybercrime surge fuels court backlog as JSC steps up recruitment – Zimbabwe News Now A new magistrate takes her oath in front of Chief Magistrate Vongai Guwuriro Muchuchuti HARARE — A growing number of drug-related and cybercrime cases is piling pressure on Zimbabwe’s magistrates’ courts, prompting the Judicial [...] The post Drug and cybercrime surge fuels court backlog as JSC steps up recruitment appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Zimbabwe backs AfCFTA agri-trade plan to boost regional agricultural exports
item.description: Zimbabwe has thrown its weight behind the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Agri-trade Action Plan, describing the strategy as closely aligned with the country’s drive to expand agricultural production, agro-industrialisation and regional trade. The strategy seeks to accelerate intra-African agricultural trade by closing the gap between production and market access. It prioritises strategic agricultural [...] The post Zimbabwe backs AfCFTA agri-trade plan to boost regional agricultural exports appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe opposition slams Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3 as ‘death of democracy’, vows court challenge
item.description: Zimbabwe’s opposition has condemned the newly enacted Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3, describing it as a major setback for democracy and vowing to challenge the law in the Constitutional Court. President Emmerson Mnangagwa on Tuesday assented to Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, paving the way for constitutional changes that include extending the terms of Parliament [...] The post Zimbabwe opposition slams Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3 as ‘death of democracy’, vows court challenge appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Corruption still holding back investment despite Zimbabwe’s progress – EU envoy says
item.description: EUROPEAN Union Ambassador to Zimbabwe Katrin Hagemann says the country has made notable progress in fighting corruption, but persistent graft continues to undermine investor confidence and prevent it from fully unlocking its economic potential. Speaking at the Integrity and Accountability Summit organised by the Transparency International Zimbabwe (TIZ) in Harare, Hagemann said integrity must move [...] The post Corruption still holding back investment despite Zimbabwe’s progress – EU envoy says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Harare traffic jams will only worsen – Mafume urges residents to ditch private cars
item.description: HARARE Mayor Jacob Mafume has renewed calls for residents to embrace public transport, saying that the capital’s worsening traffic congestion will only deteriorate unless more people abandon private vehicles for buses. Speaking at the Transparent International Zimbabwe (TI-Z) Integrity and Accountability Summit Tuesday, Mafume said the city was rapidly becoming unnavigable as the number of [...] The post Harare traffic jams will only worsen – Mafume urges residents to ditch private cars appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Powertel, Paratus activate cross-border fibre network linking Zimbabwe to Southern Africa
item.description: Powertel Communications has taken a major step towards becoming a leading Internet Service Provider (ISP) after the first phase of its landmark cross-border fibre partnership with Paratus Zimbabwe went live. The project has activated a new high-capacity digital corridor linking Zimbabwe with Botswana, Zambia and South Africa through the wider Paratus network across Southern Africa. [...] The post Powertel, Paratus activate cross-border fibre network linking Zimbabwe to Southern Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Ghana postpones Ramaphosa visit as xenophobia tensions strain ties with South Africa @CyrilRamaphosa
item.description: HARARE – Ghana has postponed South African President Cyril Ramaphosa’s planned state visit, citing heightened diplomatic sensitivities following recent anti-immigrant protests in South Africa that have led to the repatriation of hundreds of Ghanaian nationals. The visit, originally scheduled for the first week of August, had been expected to provide an opportunity for the two [...] The post Ghana postpones Ramaphosa visit as xenophobia tensions strain ties with South Africa appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe Deepens Lithium Beneficiation Push with Modern Ore Testing Facilities
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe is upgrading its mineral testing infrastructure in a renewed effort to curb revenue leakages from lithium exports, improve transparency in mineral valuation and maximise earnings from one of the country’s fastest-growing mining sectors. According to Business Insider Africa, the government is investing in modern ore-testing laboratories to strengthen the accuracy of mineral [...] The post Zimbabwe Deepens Lithium Beneficiation Push with Modern Ore Testing Facilities appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Air Zimbabwe to resume London flights @bulawayo24
item.description: Air Zimbabwe is set to resume direct flights between Harare and London by the end of July, ending nearly 15 years without a scheduled service on one of its flagship international routes.Information Mi... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Mnangagwa signs Constitutional Amendment Bill into law @bulawayo24
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has officially signed into law the controversial Constitution of Zimbabwe (Amendment) Act (No. 3), 2026, ushering in sweeping constitutional changes that extend presidenti... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: CAB3 Is Now Law: Zimbabwe Did Not Just Lose a Constitutional Battle, It Exposed Its Own Apathy @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: By Samuel Musarika President Emmerson Mnangagwa has signed CAB3 into law. We expected this to happen. ZANU-PF wanted it, ZANU-PF pushed it, ZANU-PF dressed it in constitutional language, and ZANU-PF has now delivered what it always intended to deliver. The gymnastics around words such as “constitutionalism”, “amendment”, “reform” and “legal process” were never convincing. This [...] (via The Zimbabwe Daily) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Daily

item.title: Biti blasts Mnangagwa’s Constitution Amendment No. 3 after it becomes law @nehandaradio
item.description: Tendai Biti has fiercely criticised Constitution Amendment No. 3 after President Emmerson Mnangagwa signed it into law, warning it centralises power and threatens Zimbabwe’s democracy. The post Biti blasts Mnangagwa’s Constitution Amendment No. 3 after it becomes law appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: WATCH: Coach Justin Sammons on Zimbabwe’s New “Ruthless” Culture & 2027 World Cup Vision @3menonaboat
item.description:
Zimbabwe head coach Justin Sammons delivers a candid, deep-dive tactical press conference following the first ODI against Bangladesh at the Harare Sports Club.

The post WATCH: Coach Justin Sammons on Zimbabwe’s New “Ruthless” Culture & 2027 World Cup Vision first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: 8 years after vowing to respect term limits, Mnangagwa signs law extending his rule @nehandaradio
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has signed Constitution Amendment No. 3 into law, introducing sweeping constitutional changes despite his 2018 pledge to leave office after 10 years. The post 8 years after vowing to respect term limits, Mnangagwa signs law extending his rule appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Exclusive: Air Zimbabwe Rebuilds Its Booking Engine Before London, in Silence
item.description: Days before its London return, Air Zimbabwe has switched on a new #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
